transcript: Pronto? Signor Manfredi, mi perdoni, ma non posso perdere tempo al telefono con lei. Allora, io l'anno scorso con la vostra azienda ho vinto un concorso di valigie e ancora mi devono arrivare. Ah no, non mi dica bugie, il set di valigie risulta consegnato. Ecco, allora lo ammette che era un set. A me di queste sette me ne sono arrivate solo tre. Ma mancano quattro valiggia!

speakers.name: Speaker1
speakers.voice_reasoning: The voice is animated and becomes increasingly loud and frustrated, which fits the lively and emotional delivery of an angry customer. Sadachbia captures this dynamic energy well.
speakers.style_direction: An exasperated Italian man on the phone with customer service. He starts by trying to explain his problem but quickly loses his patience, his voice rising in volume and frustration.

speakers.name: Speaker2
speakers.voice_reasoning: The speaker's tone is firm, professional, and slightly condescending, fitting a confident but unhelpful agent. Kore's firm and confident delivery is a good match for this archetype.
speakers.style_direction: A professional but unhelpful customer service representative. She speaks clearly and formally, but with an underlying tone of impatience and dismissal, as if she's reading from a script and wants to end the call.

tagged_transcript:
Speaker1: Hello?
Speaker2: [like a dismissive call center agent, speaking quickly] Mr. Manfredi, sorry, par main aapke saath phone pe time waste nahi kar sakti.
Speaker1: [getting annoyed] Suniye, pichhle saal maine aapki company ka contest jeeta tha, luggage set abhi tak nahi aaya.
Speaker2: [firmly and condescendingly] Nahi, jhoot mat boliye, luggage set delivered show ho raha hai.
Speaker1: [sarcastically triumphant] Toh aap maan gaye ki woh ek set tha. [growing more frustrated] Us saat mein se mujhe sirf teen mile hain. [shouting with exasperation] Chaar suitcase missing hain!
original_tagged_transcript:
Speaker1: Pronto?
Speaker2: [like a dismissive call center agent, speaking quickly] Signor Manfredi, mi perdoni, ma non posso perdere tempo al telefono con lei.
Speaker1: [getting annoyed] Allora, io l'anno scorso con la vostra azienda ho vinto un concorso di valigie e ancora mi devono arrivare.
Speaker2: [firmly and condescendingly] Ah no, non mi dica bugie, il set di valigie risulta consegnato.
Speaker1: [sarcastically triumphant] Ecco, allora lo ammette che era un set. [growing more frustrated] A me di queste sette me ne sono arrivate solo tre. [shouting with exasperation] Ma mancano quattro valiggia!
